mechanism_narrative: A downward or stable federal funds rate keeps discount rates compressed and supports equity multiples, so SPX forward returns are positive over the next month when FEDFUNDS is unchanged or falling from an already low level.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in triggered low-or-falling FEDFUNDS periods, or if the signal's rolling 3-year hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor income and weaker future consumption, which pressures earnings expectations and produces negative SPX returns over the next month when UNRATE accelerates upward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of UNRATE-acceleration episodes, or if the signal loses sign consistency in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales and housing starts both improve together, the data imply broad demand strengthening rather than a one-off sector effect, and that joint upturn supports positive SPX returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous RSAFS and HOUST improvement does not predict positive 60-day SPX returns, or if either proxy alone explains the effect as well as the joint signal.

mechanism_narrative: A broad industrial production contraction indicates falling real activity and margin stress, so SPX returns are negative over the next month after INDPRO declines from its recent level.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not negative after INDPRO breaks below trend, or if the signal becomes positive in a majority of such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Falling housing starts after a recent peak signal weaker construction demand and spillovers into cyclicals, so SPX underperforms over the next month when HOUST drops sharply.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by at least 5% versus the prior 2-month average
failure_condition: Reject if sharp HOUST declines are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the effect disappears after one rolling year.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation running hot while policy rates stay near zero preserves a negative real-rate backdrop that supports risk assets, so SPX forward returns are positive over the next week when CPI exceeds the policy rate by a wide margin.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL is rising while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if high inflation with near-zero FEDFUNDS does not coincide with positive 5-day SPX returns, or if the joint effect is weaker than either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: A payrolls rebound paired with falling unemployment shows improving labor market breadth and supports near-term earnings confidence, generating positive SPX returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS gains and UNRATE declines fail to predict positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not statistically distinguishable from zero in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising GDP and retail sales together indicate demand normalization that lifts earnings expectations, so SPX returns are positive over the next quarter when both series are improving from prior lows.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous GDPC1 and RSAFS improvement does not precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal reverses sign outside recovery regimes.
